cases.citation: K.M. Nanavati v. State of Maharashtra (1962)
cases.summary: Established parameters for grave and sudden provocation defense in homicide cases.
cases.significance: Crucial for determining culpable homicide vs murder.

cases.citation: Bachan Singh v. State of Punjab (1980)
cases.summary: Formulated the 'rarest of rare cases' doctrine for awarding the death sentence.
cases.significance: Governs judicial sentencing guidelines in murder cases.

cases.summary: Clarified that actual physical injury is not necessary to constitute an attempt to murder, provided intent and act are present.
cases.significance: Establishes standard of proof for attempt charges.

cases.citation: K.N. Mehra v. State of Rajasthan (1957)
cases.summary: Confirmed that temporary deprivation of property is sufficient to constitute theft; permanent intention to deprive is not required.
cases.significance: Defines parameters of possession and dishonest intent.

cases.citation: Lalita Toppo v. State of Jharkhand (2018)
cases.summary: Ruled that live-in partners can claim maintenance under the Domestic Violence Act, 2005.

cases.citation: State of Haryana v. Bhajan Lal (1992)
cases.summary: Guidelines on quashing of FIRs where allegations do not prima facie constitute cheating or fraud.

cases.citation: State of Punjab v. Baldev Singh (1999)
cases.summary: Declared that compliance with search provisions under Section 50 of the NDPS Act is mandatory.
cases.significance: Secures admissibility standards of seized contraband.
